title: Bias
objective: Detect and reduce unjustified bias across the lifecycle; document residual risks and mitigations.

title: Robustness
objective: Ensure models withstand distribution shift and adversarial conditions; enable predictable fail-safe behavior.

title: Explainability
objective: Provide audience-appropriate transparency and interpretability; disclose limits to support responsible use and governance.

title: Datasets & Tagging
objective: Protect training/inference data integrity and lawful use through provenance, licensing, sensitivity labeling, and integrity controls.

title: AI-Specific Attack Vectors
objective: Mitigate prompt injection, data poisoning, model exfiltration/theft, jailbreaks, and abuse.

title: AI Training
objective: Educate all stakeholders on ethical, secure, and responsible AI development and use aligned with organizational policies and regulatory requirements.

title: Cybersecurity Training
objective: Establish foundational and advanced security awareness to mitigate phishing, social-engineering, malware, and insider threats.

title: Governance Training
objective: Develop cross-functional literacy among leadership, risk owners, and committees in AI ethics, cybersecurity governance, and compliance management.

title: Classification & Handling
objective: Safeguard organizational and AI-related data through classification, labeling, and handling protocols aligned with sensitivity and regulatory requirements.

title: Cryptographic Management
objective: Ensure confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of data and AI models through modern, approved cryptographic controls.

title: Endpoint Security
objective: Harden and continuously monitor endpoints (user devices, servers, containers, edge nodes) to prevent malware, credential theft, and data loss, including protection of local model caches and embeddings.

title: Identity Management
objective: Ensure that only authorized, authenticated, and accountable entities—human or machine—can access systems, datasets, and AI resources, enforcing least-privilege and separation of duties.

title: Network Security
objective: Design and enforce Zero-Trust network architecture that prevents lateral movement, detects exfiltration, and protects AI workloads, APIs, and data paths.

title: Privacy Controls
objective: Protect personal and sensitive information by embedding privacy-enhancing technologies and governance in all systems.

title: Platform Security
objective: Secure cloud, container, and MLOps platforms through hardened configuration, provenance, and runtime protection to safeguard build, deploy, and model management planes.

title: Access Controls
objective: Prevent unauthorized physical access to facilities, secure areas, and media.

title: Environment Controls
objective: Protect assets against environmental hazards and facility failures.

title: Safety & Security
objective: Protect personnel and assets during incidents; ensure safe evacuation and secure handling of sensitive media.

title: Resilience Controls
objective: Engineer systems and AI workloads to resist, absorb, recover, and adapt to disruptive events, ensuring graceful degradation and mission continuity.

title: Business Continuity
objective: Maintain continuity of critical services through BIA-driven strategies, manual workarounds, and communications.

title: Disaster Recovery
objective: Restore systems, data, and AI pipelines to defined target RTO/RPO using immutable backups and tested restore procedures.

title: Supply Chain
objective: Ensure provenance, integrity, and security of third-party components, including AI models/APIs and open source.

title: Secure Development
objective: Integrate security and AI-specific assurance into the SDLC, from design and coding through build, test, release, and operations.
